statement: For every graph H with chromatic number r+1 ≥ 3 there exists N such that every n-vertex H-free graph with n > N has at most as many edges as the Turán graph T(n,r).

proposed_edges.rationale: The conjecture asserts that the asymptotic density supplied by Erdős–Stone is achieved exactly by the Turán graph for all sufficiently large n.
When H is itself a clique the statement reduces to the classical uniqueness part of Turán’s theorem for large n.

why_interesting: It upgrades the proven asymptotic result of Erdős–Stone to an exact extremal statement, a strengthening that remains open for arbitrary forbidden subgraphs.

statement: For every graph H with chromatic number r+1 ≥ 3 that possesses at least one edge e with χ(H−e)=r, there exists N such that every n-vertex H-free graph with n>N has at most as many edges as T(n,r).

proposed_edges.rationale: The new conjecture strengthens the original by restricting to color-critical H, for which the exact Turán bound is expected to hold.
When H is itself a clique it reduces exactly to the classical uniqueness statement of Turán’s theorem for large n.

why_interesting: It isolates the precise structural condition on H under which the asymptotic density supplied by Erdős–Stone is realized exactly by the Turán graph, separating known true cases from suspected counterexamples.

statement: For every graph H with chromatic number r+1 ≥ 3 possessing at least one edge e with χ(H−e)=r, there exists N such that the unique n-vertex H-free graph on n>N attaining the maximum number of edges is the Turán graph T(n,r).

proposed_edges.rationale: The uniqueness statement strengthens the exact bound by characterizing all extremal graphs rather than merely bounding their size.
When H is a clique the statement reduces exactly to the classical uniqueness part of Turán’s theorem for large n.

why_interesting: It isolates the structural rigidity that color-critical edges are expected to impose on extremal graphs, separating them from merely asymptotic density results.

statement: For any non-bipartite graph H with chromatic number r+1 that is not a clique, the extremal function satisfies ex(n, H) = e(T(n,r)) + O(n^{2-ε}) for some ε=ε(H)>0.

proposed_edges.rationale: Provides a quantitative strengthening of the asymptotic bound in Erdős–Stone by improving the o(n²) error term when H is not a clique.

why_interesting: Bridges the exact Turán case (cliques) with the asymptotic Erdős–Stone regime by predicting polynomial savings in the error term for all other forbidden subgraphs.

statement: The extremal number ex(n, C_4) equals (1/2 + o(1)) n^{3/2}.

proposed_edges.rationale: Provides the precise leading constant in the bipartite forbidden-subgraph regime where Erdős–Stone supplies only the trivial o(n²) bound.

why_interesting: Bridges the asymptotic regime of Erdős–Stone (chromatic number ≥3) to the much harder bipartite extremal problem by predicting the exact order for the simplest even cycle.

statement: For every n, the maximum number of edges in an n-vertex Petersen-free graph is at most (1/2+o(1))n^{3/2}.

proposed_edges.rationale: The conjecture directly bounds the extremal number ex(n,Petersen) and is therefore conditioned on the Petersen graph.
The proposed bound matches the Bondy–Simonovits order for C_6 and C_8, both subgraphs of the Petersen graph.
The statement supplies an explicit asymptotic upper bound on the extremal function ex(n,P).

why_interesting: The Petersen graph is triangle-free and C_4-free yet has chromatic number 3, so the conjecture bridges the even-cycle extremal regime with the chromatic Turán regime in a quantitatively sharper way than Erdős–Stone.

statement: For every fixed integer k ≥ 2, the extremal number ex(n, M_k) where M_k denotes the k-th Mycielski graph satisfies ex(n, M_k) = (1 - 1/(χ(M_k)-1) + o(1)) n²/2, with the Turán graph T(n, χ(M_k)-1) being the unique extremal graph for all sufficiently large n.

proposed_edges.rationale: The conjecture strengthens the asymptotic statement of Erdős–Stone by asserting that the extremal graphs are exactly the Turán graphs even when the forbidden graph is triangle-free but has arbitrarily large chromatic number.

Mycielski graphs provide an infinite family of K_3-free graphs with χ(M_k) arbitrarily large, making the exact version of Erdős–Stone for these specific graphs a natural and previously unstated special case.
The conjecture is explicitly conditioned on the family of Mycielski graphs.

why_interesting: It would show that high chromatic number alone, even in the absence of triangles, forces the extremal function and extremal graphs to coincide with the Turán construction, bridging chromatic extremal problems with classical Turán theory in a surprising way.

statement: For every integer k ≥ 2 the extremal number ex(n, W_k) where W_k denotes the windmill graph consisting of k triangles sharing a common vertex satisfies ex(n, W_k) = (k-1)n/2 + o(n) and is achieved by the disjoint union of cliques of order 2k-1.

proposed_edges.rationale: The proposed bound recovers the Erdős–Gallai path extremal function when k=1 and supplies an analogous linear-density extremal result for the windmill family.
The conjecture is explicitly conditioned on the windmill graphs that appear in the Friendship Theorem.

why_interesting: It would give the first exact asymptotic extremal function for an infinite family of graphs whose chromatic number is 3 yet which are not covered by the Erdős–Stone or Erdős–Gallai frameworks.

statement: For every k ≥ 3 the extremal number satisfies ex(n, C_{2k}) = (1/2 + o(1)) n^{1 + 1/k} with the leading constant determined by the (asymptotic) extremal density of the corresponding Zarankiewicz problem z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: The conjecture supplies the precise leading constant missing from the Θ-notation of Bondy–Simonovits while preserving the same polynomial order.
When k = 2 the statement reduces exactly to the Zarankiewicz problem for K_{2,2} ≅ C_4.

why_interesting: It bridges the asymptotic Bondy–Simonovits theorem with the still-open Zarankiewicz constants, predicting that the algebraic constructions known to be optimal for C_4 remain asymptotically optimal for all even cycles.

statement: For every integer k ≥ 2, ex(n, C_{2k+1}) = (1/2 + o(1)) n^{1 + 1/k} and the leading constant is determined by the asymptotic density of the extremal Zarankiewicz problem z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: The proposed statement supplies matching asymptotic order and leading-constant description for odd cycles, exactly analogous to the even-cycle case already present.
When k=2 the statement reduces to the same Zarankiewicz density that governs C_4.

why_interesting: It would close the long-standing gap between the known polynomial upper bounds for odd-cycle extremal numbers and the precise constants furnished by Zarankiewicz theory, unifying even- and odd-cycle problems.

statement: For every s,t ≥ 2 the extremal number satisfies ex(n,K_{s,t}) = (1/2 + o(1)) n^{2-1/s} z(n;s,t) where the leading constant is exactly the asymptotic density of the Zarankiewicz problem z(n;s,t).

proposed_edges.rationale: The conjecture supplies the precise leading constant (via the Zarankiewicz density) that is missing from the O-notation upper bound of KST while preserving the same polynomial order.
When H = K_{s,t} the statement is precisely the exact-density version of the asymptotic bound asserted by the bipartite Turán conjecture.

why_interesting: It bridges the well-studied asymptotic KST theorem with the still-open Zarankiewicz problem by predicting that the extremal density is completely governed by the corresponding Zarankiewicz constant, a natural but unproven tightening at the boundary of known results.

statement: For every integer k ≥ 2 the maximum number of edges in an n-vertex graph of girth larger than 2k equals (1/2 + o(1)) n^{1 + 1/k}, with the leading constant given by the asymptotic density of the Zarankiewicz problem z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: The girth statement simultaneously forbids all even cycles of length at most 2k and therefore implies the even-cycle extremal bound while adding the stronger girth hypothesis.

Forbidding only a single even cycle C_{2k} yields the same asymptotic order; the girth conjecture strengthens the forbidden family yet retains the identical leading-term prediction via the same Zarankiewicz density.
When k = 2 the girth condition reduces exactly to C_4-freeness, recovering the Zarankiewicz problem for K_{2,2}.

why_interesting: It supplies a single precise asymptotic for the classical (n,g)-problem that unifies all even-cycle results while remaining open even for girth 8.

statement: For every integer k ≥ 2, the maximum number of edges in an n-vertex graph whose odd girth exceeds 2k+1 equals (1/2 + o(1)) n^{1 + 1/k}, with the leading constant given by the asymptotic density of the Zarankiewicz problem z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: Forbidding every odd cycle of length at most 2k+1 simultaneously is a strictly stronger hypothesis than forbidding a single odd cycle C_{2k+1}, yet the conjecture asserts the identical asymptotic order and leading constant.

The statement supplies matching asymptotic order together with an explicit Zarankiewicz-derived constant for the family of all short odd cycles, extending the Θ-bound that Bondy–Simonovits gives for a single even cycle.
The conjecture directly predicts the precise growth rate of the extremal function under an odd-girth hypothesis.

why_interesting: It bridges the single-cycle and full-girth extremal pictures by showing that the same Zarankiewicz density governs the edge-maximization problem even when only odd cycles are forbidden.

statement: Any n-vertex graph containing more than (k-1)n/2 edges contains every tree on k+1 vertices as a subgraph.

proposed_edges.rationale: The conjecture asserts the identical extremal bound for every tree on k+1 vertices, of which the path P_{k+1} is a special case already settled by Erdős–Gallai.
The statement directly supplies the precise extremal function ex(n,T) for any tree T.
The forbidden family consists exactly of all trees of a given order.

why_interesting: It claims that the extremal number of every tree depends only on its number of vertices and matches the already-known path bound, a non-obvious uniformity result.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every pair of distinct vertices has at most one common neighbor contains at most (1/2+o(1))n^{3/2} edges, with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n;2,2).

proposed_edges.rationale: The friendship theorem settles the exactly-one case by characterizing the extremal graphs; the new statement supplies the matching asymptotic upper bound for the weaker at-most-one hypothesis.
Forbidding two common neighbors is equivalent to C_4-freeness in the square of the adjacency matrix, reducing precisely to the K_{2,2} Zarankiewicz problem.

why_interesting: It bridges the structural characterization given by the Friendship Theorem with the asymptotic extremal theory of even cycles and Zarankiewicz problems, predicting the same growth rate under a strictly weaker local hypothesis.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every pair of distinct vertices has at most r common neighbors contains at most (1/2 + o(1)) n^{3/2} sqrt(r) edges, with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n;2,r+1).

proposed_edges.rationale: The r=1 case recovers the already-stated at-most-one conjecture; the new statement supplies the natural constant-factor generalization via the same Zarankiewicz density.

Friendship settles the exactly-one (hence at-most-one) case by structural characterization; the conjecture gives the matching asymptotic upper bound once the common-neighbor multiplicity is relaxed to any fixed r.
Forbidding r+1 common neighbors is equivalent to K_{2,r+1}-freeness in the square of the adjacency matrix, reducing precisely to the (2,r+1) Zarankiewicz problem.

why_interesting: It interpolates between the friendship theorem and the full Zarankiewicz problem, predicting that the extremal order remains n^{3/2} for any fixed multiplicity bound r while the constant grows exactly like sqrt(r).

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every pair of distinct vertices has at most r common neighbors is homomorphic to a Kővári–Sós–Turán extremal graph for K_{2,r+1} and therefore has chromatic number O(n^{1/2}).

proposed_edges.rationale: The new conjecture lifts the edge-count statement of the r-common-neighbor extremal conjecture to a chromatic-number conclusion via degeneracy.
The conjecture supplies an explicit O(sqrt(n)) upper bound on chromatic number under a local neighborhood condition that complements Brooks' degree bound.

why_interesting: It connects a purely local forbidden-configuration hypothesis (bounded codegrees) directly to global coloring bounds, bridging extremal graph theory with chromatic-number questions in a quantitatively sharp way.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every triple of distinct vertices has at most one common neighbor contains at most (1/2 + o(1)) n^{4/3} edges, with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n;3,2).

new_node.name: At-Most-One Common Triple Neighbor Extremal Conjecture

proposed_edges.rationale: The new statement lifts the pairwise common-neighbor hypothesis to a 3-uniform version while preserving the same Zarankiewicz-driven asymptotic form, now of order n^{4/3}.
Forbidding two common neighbors on every triple is equivalent to K_{3,2}-freeness in the cube of the adjacency matrix, recovering the KST bound for s=3,t=2.

why_interesting: It bridges the pairwise common-neighbor extremal theory already present with higher-uniform Zarankiewicz problems, predicting a new polynomial order not implied by any existing node.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every triple of distinct vertices has at most one common neighbor has chromatic number O(n^{1/3}).

proposed_edges.rationale: The new conjecture lifts the edge-count bound of the triple common-neighbor extremal conjecture to a chromatic-number conclusion via degeneracy arguments.
The conjecture supplies an explicit O(n^{1/3}) upper bound on chromatic number under a 3-uniform local neighborhood condition.

why_interesting: It bridges the 3-uniform Zarankiewicz-type extremal statement to coloring bounds, paralleling the pairwise case while operating at a different polynomial scale.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every set of k distinct vertices has at most one common neighbor contains at most (1/2 + o(1)) n^{1 + 1/k} edges, with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, 2).

new_node.name: At-Most-One k-Set Common Neighbor Extremal Conjecture

proposed_edges.rationale: The k=3 case recovers exactly the already-stated triple conjecture while the new statement supplies the uniform formulation for arbitrary k.
The k=2 case recovers exactly the pairwise conjecture, extending the Zarankiewicz-driven bound from pairs to arbitrary uniform set sizes.
Forbidding two common neighbors on every k-set is equivalent to K_{k,2}-freeness in the k-th power of the adjacency matrix, recovering the KST bound.

why_interesting: It supplies a single Zarankiewicz-governed asymptotic that simultaneously generalizes all existing common-neighbor extremal statements from fixed set size 2 or 3 to arbitrary uniformity while preserving the same proof technique via higher-order incidence graphs.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every set of k distinct vertices has at most one common neighbor has chromatic number O(n^{1/k}).

proposed_edges.rationale: The new conjecture lifts the edge-count bound of the k-set common-neighbor extremal conjecture to a chromatic-number conclusion via degeneracy arguments, exactly as done for the special cases k=2 and k=3.
The conjecture supplies an explicit O(n^{1/k}) upper bound on chromatic number under a k-uniform local neighborhood condition.

why_interesting: It uniformly extends the known pairwise and triple chromatic conjectures to arbitrary set size k while retaining the precise Zarankiewicz exponent already verified for the edge extremal function.

statement: Any n-vertex graph of girth larger than 2k is homomorphic to a Kővári–Sós–Turán extremal graph for K_{k,k} and therefore has chromatic number O(n^{1/k}).

proposed_edges.rationale: The chromatic conjecture lifts the edge-count bound under a girth hypothesis to a degeneracy-derived chromatic-number conclusion via the same Zarankiewicz density.
Large girth forbids short closed walks and therefore implies the k-uniform common-neighbor condition used in the earlier chromatic conjecture while preserving the O(n^{1/k}) bound.

why_interesting: It bridges the girth-extremal conjectures (already tied to Zarankiewicz) with the common-neighbor chromatic conjectures, predicting that high-girth graphs are degenerate in exactly the same polynomial regime.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every pair of distinct vertices has at most one common neighbor has independence number Omega(sqrt(n)), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n;2,2).

proposed_edges.rationale: An Omega(sqrt(n)) independence-number lower bound is the natural dual consequence of the O(sqrt(n)) chromatic-number upper bound already conjectured under the same codegree-1 hypothesis.
The codegree-1 condition is equivalent to C_4-freeness in the square of the adjacency matrix and therefore reduces exactly to the same Zarankiewicz density that governs the edge-extremal statement.

why_interesting: It supplies a direct combinatorial lower bound on the independence number that complements the existing chromatic-number conjecture without passing through degeneracy arguments, while remaining asymptotically tight by the same Zarankiewicz constructions.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every set of k distinct vertices has at most one common neighbor has independence number Omega(n^{k/(k+1)}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, 2).

proposed_edges.rationale: An Omega(n^{k/(k+1)}) independence-number lower bound is the natural dual consequence of the O(n^{1+1/k}) edge upper bound already conjectured under the same k-uniform common-neighbor hypothesis.
The k=2 case recovers exactly the already-stated pairwise independence conjecture, extending the Zarankiewicz-driven bound from pairs to arbitrary uniform set sizes.

why_interesting: The statement supplies the matching independence-number dual to the existing family of common-neighbor extremal conjectures, bridging edge-extremal and Ramsey-type questions at the same Zarankiewicz densities.

statement: Any n-vertex graph of girth larger than 2k has independence number Omega(n^{(k-1)/k}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: An Omega(n^{(k-1)/k}) independence lower bound is the direct dual consequence of the O(n^{1/k}) chromatic upper bound already conjectured under the same girth hypothesis.
The independence statement follows from the edge-extremal bound via the standard degeneracy argument that converts an O(n^{1+1/k}) edge count into an Omega(n^{(k-1)/k}) independent set.
When k=2 the girth hypothesis reduces to C_4-freeness and recovers the same Zarankiewicz density that governs the independence number of K_{2,2}-free graphs.

why_interesting: It supplies the natural dual independence-number consequence of the already-stated girth chromatic conjecture, thereby completing the chromatic-independence sandwich for graphs of large girth via Zarankiewicz densities.

statement: Any n-vertex graph whose odd girth exceeds 2k+1 is homomorphic to a Kővári–Sós–Turán extremal graph for K_{k,k} and therefore has chromatic number O(n^{1/k}).

proposed_edges.rationale: The chromatic conjecture lifts the edge-count bound under an odd-girth hypothesis to a degeneracy-derived chromatic-number conclusion via the same Zarankiewicz density.
Odd girth larger than 2k+1 is a strictly weaker hypothesis than girth larger than 2k, yet the conjecture asserts the identical O(n^{1/k}) chromatic bound.

why_interesting: It bridges the odd-girth extremal conjecture (already present) with chromatic-number consequences, extending the even/odd girth dichotomy already visible in the edge-extremal setting to degeneracy and coloring.

statement: Any n-vertex graph whose odd girth exceeds 2k+1 has independence number Omega(n^{(k-1)/k}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: An Omega(n^{(k-1)/k}) independence lower bound is the direct dual consequence of the O(n^{1/k}) chromatic upper bound already conjectured under the same odd-girth hypothesis.
The independence statement follows from the edge-extremal bound via the standard degeneracy argument that converts an O(n^{1+1/k}) edge count into an Omega(n^{(k-1)/k}) independent set.
Odd girth larger than 2k+1 is a strictly weaker hypothesis than girth larger than 2k, yet the conjecture asserts the identical Omega(n^{(k-1)/k}) independence bound.

why_interesting: It supplies the natural dual independence-number statement for the existing odd-girth extremal and chromatic conjectures, bridging the even/odd girth gap already present for edge and chromatic bounds.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every pair of distinct vertices has at most r common neighbors has chromatic number O(sqrt(r n)), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n;2,r+1).

proposed_edges.rationale: The new conjecture lifts the O(n^{3/2} sqrt(r)) edge upper bound to an explicit O(sqrt(r n)) chromatic-number upper bound via the standard degeneracy argument.
The r=1 case recovers the already-stated common-neighbor chromatic conjecture while the new statement supplies the natural scaling with r.

why_interesting: It supplies the missing chromatic-number counterpart to the already-present edge-extremal conjecture for arbitrary fixed codegree r, unifying the r-dependent Zarankiewicz scaling across both parameters.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every pair of distinct vertices has at most r common neighbors has independence number Omega(sqrt(n/r)), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n;2,r+1).

proposed_edges.rationale: An Omega(sqrt(n/r)) independence lower bound is the natural dual consequence of the O(sqrt(r n)) chromatic upper bound already conjectured under the same at-most-r common-neighbor hypothesis.
The independence statement follows from the O(n^{3/2} sqrt(r)) edge upper bound via the standard degeneracy argument that converts an edge bound into an independent-set lower bound.

The codegree-at-most-r condition is equivalent to K_{2,r+1}-freeness in the square of the adjacency matrix and therefore reduces exactly to the same Zarankiewicz density that governs the edge-extremal statement.

why_interesting: It supplies the missing dual independence-number bound that completes the picture for the family of at-most-r common-neighbor conjectures already present for edges and chromatic number.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every pair of distinct vertices has at most r common neighbors has spectral radius O(sqrt(r n)), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n;2,r+1).

proposed_edges.rationale: The spectral-radius conjecture lifts the O(n^{3/2} sqrt(r)) edge upper bound to an explicit O(sqrt(r n)) bound on the largest eigenvalue via the standard Rayleigh-quotient or trace argument on A^2.
The conjecture supplies an explicit O(sqrt(r n)) upper bound on the largest eigenvalue under a local neighborhood condition that complements the expander mixing lemma's eigenvalue-edge relation.
An O(sqrt(r n)) spectral-radius bound immediately yields the same-order chromatic-number upper bound already conjectured via the greedy-coloring argument λ+1.

why_interesting: The spectral formulation directly encodes the codegree hypothesis via the quadratic form of A^2 and bridges the extremal-edge conjectures to spectral graph theory in a manner parallel to existing chromatic and independence statements.

statement: Any n-vertex graph of girth larger than 2k has spectral radius O(n^{1/k}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: An O(n^{1/k}) spectral-radius upper bound immediately implies the matching O(n^{1/k}) chromatic-number bound already conjectured via the greedy-coloring argument λ+1.

The spectral conjecture lifts the O(n^{1+1/k}) edge upper bound under a girth hypothesis to an explicit O(n^{1/k}) bound on the largest eigenvalue via the standard Rayleigh-quotient or trace argument on A^2.
The conjecture supplies an explicit O(n^{1/k}) upper bound on the largest eigenvalue under a girth condition that complements the expander mixing lemma's eigenvalue-edge relation.
When k=2 the girth hypothesis reduces to C_4-freeness and recovers the same Zarankiewicz density that governs the spectral radius of K_{2,2}-free graphs.

why_interesting: It supplies the missing spectral-radius analogue that unifies the existing girth-based chromatic and independence conjectures with the expander-mixing and Zarankiewicz frameworks.

statement: Any n-vertex graph whose odd girth exceeds 2k+1 has spectral radius O(n^{1/k}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: An O(n^{1/k}) spectral-radius upper bound immediately implies the matching O(n^{1/k}) chromatic-number bound already conjectured via the greedy-coloring argument λ+1.

The spectral conjecture lifts the O(n^{1+1/k}) edge upper bound under an odd-girth hypothesis to an explicit O(n^{1/k}) bound on the largest eigenvalue via the standard Rayleigh-quotient or trace argument on A^2.
The conjecture supplies an explicit O(n^{1/k}) upper bound on the largest eigenvalue under an odd-girth condition that complements the expander mixing lemma's eigenvalue-edge relation.
Odd girth larger than 2k+1 is a strictly weaker hypothesis than girth larger than 2k, yet the conjecture asserts the identical O(n^{1/k}) spectral-radius bound.

why_interesting: It supplies the natural spectral-radius counterpart to the existing odd-girth chromatic and independence conjectures, unifying eigenvalue techniques with Zarankiewicz densities under an odd-girth hypothesis.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every set of k distinct vertices has at most one common neighbor has spectral radius O(n^{1/k}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, 2).

new_node.name: k-Set Common-Neighbor Spectral Radius Bound Conjecture

proposed_edges.rationale: The spectral-radius conjecture lifts the O(n^{1+1/k}) edge upper bound under the k-uniform common-neighbor hypothesis to an explicit O(n^{1/k}) bound on the largest eigenvalue via the standard Rayleigh-quotient or trace argument on A^k.
An O(n^{1/k}) spectral-radius bound immediately yields the same-order chromatic-number upper bound already conjectured via the greedy-coloring argument λ+1.

The conjecture supplies an explicit O(n^{1/k}) upper bound on the largest eigenvalue under a k-uniform local neighborhood condition that complements the expander mixing lemma's eigenvalue-edge relation.

why_interesting: It completes the natural quartet (edges, chromatic number, independence number, spectral radius) for the k-set common-neighbor condition by supplying the missing spectral link, exactly analogous to the already-stated pair and girth cases.

statement: Any n-vertex graph of girth larger than 2k has maximum degree O(n^{1/k}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: The maximum-degree statement follows from the edge-extremal bound by observing that an O(n^{1+1/k}) edge count under the girth hypothesis forces every degree to be O(n^{1/k}) on average and, by standard counting, in the worst case.
An O(n^{1/k}) upper bound on maximum degree immediately yields the same-order upper bound on spectral radius already conjectured via the Rayleigh quotient.
The conjecture directly supplies an explicit polynomial upper bound on the maximum degree under a girth hypothesis.

why_interesting: It supplies the natural degree-theoretic counterpart to the already-present girth extremal, chromatic, independence, and spectral conjectures, all governed by the same Zarankiewicz density, and is consistent with Moore-type bounds for high-girth graphs.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every pair of distinct vertices has at most r common neighbors has maximum degree O(sqrt(r n)), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n;2,r+1).

proposed_edges.rationale: The new maximum-degree conjecture follows from the known edge-extremal conjecture via the standard counting argument that converts an O(n^{3/2} sqrt(r)) edge bound into an O(sqrt(r n)) degree bound.

When r=1 the common-neighbor hypothesis is equivalent to C_4-freeness in the square of the adjacency matrix and recovers the same Zarankiewicz density that governs the girth-derived maximum-degree bound.
The conjecture directly supplies an explicit polynomial upper bound on the maximum degree under a codegree-at-most-r hypothesis.

why_interesting: It completes the natural quartet of edge/chromatic/independence/spectral/max-degree consequences under a uniform local-neighborhood hypothesis while remaining at the precise boundary of the known Zarankiewicz-driven results.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every set of k distinct vertices has at most one common neighbor has maximum degree O(n^{1/k}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, 2).

proposed_edges.rationale: The new maximum-degree statement follows from the known edge-extremal conjecture via the standard counting argument that converts an O(n^{1+1/k}) edge bound into an O(n^{1/k}) degree bound.
The conjecture directly supplies an explicit polynomial upper bound on the maximum degree under a k-uniform common-neighbor hypothesis.
An O(n^{1/k}) upper bound on maximum degree immediately yields the same-order upper bound on spectral radius via the Rayleigh quotient.

why_interesting: It completes the natural quartet of edge, chromatic, independence, spectral, and degree bounds under the same k-uniform common-neighbor hypothesis, all governed by identical Zarankiewicz densities.

statement: Any n-vertex graph whose odd girth exceeds 2k+1 has maximum degree O(n^{1/k}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, k).

An O(n^{1/k}) upper bound on maximum degree immediately yields the same-order upper bound on spectral radius via the Rayleigh quotient.
The conjecture directly supplies an explicit polynomial upper bound on the maximum degree under an odd-girth hypothesis.

why_interesting: It completes the uniform set of five extremal parameters (edges, chromatic number, independence number, spectral radius, maximum degree) for the odd-girth setting, exactly analogous to the already-present girth case.

statement: Any n-vertex graph whose odd girth exceeds 2k+1 has maximum degree O(n^{1/k}), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n; k, k).

proposed_edges.rationale: Odd girth larger than 2k+1 is a strictly weaker hypothesis than girth larger than 2k, yet the conjecture asserts the identical O(n^{1/k}) maximum-degree bound.

why_interesting: This bridges the odd-girth and girth families of extremal conjectures in exactly the same way already done for the spectral-radius versions, exposing a uniform structural pattern across degree, spectral, chromatic and independence bounds.

statement: Any n-vertex graph in which every pair of distinct vertices has at most r common neighbors has list-chromatic number O(sqrt(r n)), with the leading constant governed by the Zarankiewicz density z(n;2,r+1).

proposed_edges.rationale: List-chromatic number is at least the ordinary chromatic number, so the conjecture strengthens the already-stated chromatic bound while preserving the same Zarankiewicz-derived order.
The new statement lifts the O(n^{3/2} sqrt(r)) edge upper bound to an explicit list-chromatic conclusion via the same degeneracy argument used for ordinary chromatic number.

why_interesting: The conjecture extends the degeneracy-derived coloring bounds to the strictly stronger list-coloring setting under a local codegree hypothesis, a step that remains open even for the classical girth case.
